title: Boat Rental in Valencia: How to Choose, Costs, 2026
html:
Choosing a boat rental in Valencia starts at Marina de Valencia, the old America's Cup harbour beside the city, where a day on the water runs from roughly EUR 90 for a shared trip to EUR 1,200 for a crewed yacht. BoatHire24 lists verified vessels along this Spanish Mediterranean coast.

How do you choose a boat to rent in Valencia?

Match the boat to your group size, your budget and how far you want to travel along the coast. A couple wanting a quiet morning needs something very different from a party of ten. Begin with the number of guests, then decide between a calm harbour cruise and a faster run south toward the Albufera or north to the Malvarrosa shoreline. Wind and swell off the Gulf of Valencia stay moderate through summer, so most well-found motorboats handle the day comfortably.

Where to set off in Valencia

Valencia gives you several genuine departure points, each with its own character and parking.

  • Marina de Valencia: the central harbour by the City of Arts and Sciences, the main hub for charters.
  • Marina Real Juan Carlos I: the former America's Cup base, with deep water and larger berths.
  • Port Saplaya: a small, colourful marina just north, nicknamed the little Venice of Valencia.
  • El Saler: a village fringing the Albufera lagoon to the south, near sheltered bays.
  • Pobla de Farnals: a quieter marina up the coast, useful for avoiding the city crowds.

Which boat to rent in Valencia

The right hull depends on whether you want speed, shade or space to lounge, so weigh the options carefully.

  1. Open speedboat: best for quick coastal runs and swimming stops, seats up to 6, from EUR 250 for a half-day.
  2. RIB: a stable, splashy all-rounder for families, seats up to 8, from EUR 300 a day.
  3. Sailing yacht: ideal for an unhurried day under canvas, seats up to 8, from EUR 450 a day.
  4. Catamaran: roomy and steady for groups and sundowners, seats up to 12, from EUR 900 a day.
  5. Crewed motor yacht: a captained day with full comfort, seats up to 10, from EUR 1,200 a day.

If you favour speed and an easy ladder for swimming, a nimble craft like this speedboat suits short hops along the Valencia coast better than a heavy displacement hull.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Valencia?

Prices in Valencia scale with the size of the boat, the length of the trip and whether a skipper is included. You can rent a boat for your exact dates and then weigh the bands below against your group.

  • A shared group tour from Marina de Valencia starts at about EUR 90 per person.
  • A small motorboat for a half-day costs from around EUR 250 in EUR.
  • A catamaran for a full day runs from roughly EUR 900.
  • A crewed yacht for the day starts near EUR 1,200, fuel and skipper usually extra.

When is the best season to rent a boat in Valencia?

High season runs June to September, with warm, settled water and long daylight for full-day trips. Spring and early autumn stay pleasant and often cheaper, while winter charters are limited. The local easterly wind, the Llevant, can build a short chop on exposed afternoons, so morning starts are calmer. Spain follows the principle that larger and faster boats may require a recognised licence; the operator confirms the exact requirements for each vessel.

Book a morning slot in July or August: the sea off the Malvarrosa is glassy at dawn, and the Llevant wind that ruffles the Gulf of Valencia usually arrives only after midday.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 90 shared to EUR 1,200 crewed per day.
  • Peak months: June to September.
  • Typical trip length: 4 to 8 hours.
  • Capacity: 2 to 12 guests depending on the boat.
  • Currency: EUR.

Questions about boat rental in Valencia

Do I need a licence to rent a boat in Valencia?

For smaller motorboats within set power limits you can usually hire without a formal licence in Spain, while larger or faster yachts require one. The operator confirms the exact rules and paperwork for each boat before you sail.

How far can I sail in a day from Valencia?

A typical day trip covers the coast within roughly 15 nautical miles of the marina, reaching the Albufera shoreline to the south or Port Saplaya to the north with time to swim and return before dusk.

Is a skipper included when I rent a boat in Valencia?

Many larger boats come with a professional skipper, and some smaller ones can be hired bareboat if you hold the right licence. Each listing states clearly whether a captain is included in the quoted price.

Picking the right boat in Valencia comes down to matching guests, budget and distance, then booking early in the 2026 season for the calmest mornings. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Valencia or list your own, so browse the listings to compare verified vessels and skippers for your dates.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Valencia and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
